local_validation.notes: The first generic execution attempt exposed an implicit-call scanner bug: Push(m_hResult) inside m_hResultStack.Push(m_hResult) was treated as a self call. The scanner now tracks the previous token and skips implicit calls preceded by '.'. The proof uses node_eq for blueprint comparison so Go, Rust, and TypeScript agree.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A .bml zero-argument constructor assignment parsed from the thesis companion corpus can now create a typed native record and assign it to self state.
test_flows: python3 scripts/audit_bml_bmf_kernel_natives.py before commit to prove no new BML/BMF host-native semantics were introduced.

phase_gate.reason: This adds TypeName() constructor assignment. Full thesis parity still requires receiver calls with arguments, nested call arguments, conditionals, loops, casts, comparisons, exceptions, and full BMF rule execution from the known-working companion code.

evidence_refs: form/form-stdlib/grammars/bml.fk defines constructor-assignment detection and TypeName() record construction in the BML language layer.
form/form-stdlib/tests/bml-thesis-bmf-start-result-stack-constructor-assignment-proof.fk proves m_hResult = ParseStack(); from original BMF-grammar.bml executes against native record state.
The same change tightens implicit self-call detection so receiver-call arguments are not mistaken for self calls.
